steps.title: Compare the two ideas
steps.content:
Sentence 1: barnacle movement has been attributed to passive displacement by water currents.

Sentence 2: a study found barnacles moved against the prevailing water flow, consistent with self-initiated locomotion.

The new finding contradicts the old explanation — barnacles can't be passively pushed against the current.

steps.title: Pick the contrast transition
steps.content:
A (Confirming) and C (similar conclusion) wrongly suggest agreement. B (Contrary to this phenomenon) is illogical — the new finding is contrary to the explanation, not the phenomenon itself.

Only D (Undermining this explanation) captures that the study weakens the passive-displacement hypothesis. D is correct.
